title: Structuring / Smurfing
description: Breaking down large transactions into smaller amounts to avoid Currency Transaction Report (CTR) thresholds. Also known as smurfing when multiple individuals are used.

title: Circular Trading / Round-Tripping
description: Funds moving through multiple accounts in a circular pattern only to return to the original source or beneficiary, obscuring the money trail through layering.

title: Rapid Movement of Funds
description: Funds deposited and immediately withdrawn or transferred at unusually high velocity, leaving no economic rationale. Indicative of layering in money laundering.

title: High-Risk Jurisdiction Transactions
description: Transactions involving countries identified by FATF as having strategic deficiencies in their AML/CFT regimes, requiring enhanced due diligence.

title: Suspicious Transaction Reporting
description: Every banking company shall furnish suspicious transaction reports to the Financial Intelligence Unit-India (FIU-IND) within 7 days of detection. Failure to report is an offence under Section 13.

title: Cash Transaction Report Threshold
description: Cash transactions exceeding Rs. 10 lakhs or its equivalent in foreign currency must be reported. Structuring transactions below this threshold to avoid reporting is a criminal offence.

title: Offence of Money Laundering
description: Whosoever directly or indirectly attempts to indulge in or knowingly assists or is a party to any process or activity connected with proceeds of crime shall be guilty of money laundering.

title: Penalties for Non-Compliance
description: Failure to maintain records, furnish information, or verify identity of clients shall be punishable with a fine which shall not be less than ten thousand rupees but may extend to one lakh rupees for each failure.

title: Wire Transfer Regulations
description: Countries should ensure that financial institutions include required and accurate originator information and required beneficiary information on wire transfers and related messages.

title: Customer Due Diligence
section: FATF Recommendation 10 / RBI KYC Master Direction 2016
description: Financial institutions should identify and verify the identity of customers, beneficial owners, and conduct ongoing due diligence. Enhanced due diligence for high-risk customers.

title: Record Keeping Requirements
description: Every reporting entity shall maintain records of all transactions including domestic and international for a period of five years from the date of transaction.

title: FinCEN Advisory on Smurfing
description: Advisory on detecting and reporting structured transactions where multiple individuals (smurfs) conduct transactions on behalf of others to evade BSA reporting and recordkeeping requirements.
